notes.note: "Sampsiceramus" is Cicero's mocking nickname for Pompey, after Sampsiceramus the petty Arab dynast of Emesa whom Pompey had dealt with in the East. It needles Pompey's Eastern conquests and his oriental, would-be-monarchic airs.

notes.anchor: the kinsman of our Ox-Eyed Hera
notes.note: A double riddle: the "Ox-Eyed Hera" (Greek boopis, Homer's epithet for the goddess) is Clodia, scandalously rumoured to be intimate with her brother, and her "kinsman" is therefore P. Clodius Pulcher. Clodius is now openly threatening Cicero with his coming tribunate.
